root_motivation: Replace ad-hoc bash drafts of `claude-self` with a Rust CLI that maintains a stable `CLAUDE_SELF.md` (voice/values/defaults) at `~/.claude/CLAUDE_SELF.md`. The lint contract (canonical sections, length cap, non-empty Aspirations, no duplicate bullets) is the load-bearing piece — without it the file rots into a bland, drift-prone catch-all and the per-session 'self model' reassembly remains lossy.
user_persona: Single developer (Joe) co-editing a 7-section markdown file with Claude on one laptop. Runs `claude-self lint` to validate after edits, `claude-self show` to inspect, `claude-self diff`/`log` to track history, `claude-self default-restore` to recover.

unfakeable_metric.name: acceptance_tests_passing
unfakeable_metric.harness_command: cargo test --workspace --test 'acceptance_*' -- --format json -Z unstable-options 2>/dev/null | grep -c '"event":"ok"' || true

acceptance_criteria.description: `claude-self path` prints the resolved live-file path (default `$HOME/.claude/CLAUDE_SELF.md`, overridable via `CLAUDE_SELF_FILE`) to stdout and exits 0.
`claude-self show` writes the live file's contents to stdout when present (exit 0); exits 2 with diagnostic on stderr when missing.

`claude-self lint` enforces the four lint rules: (a) exactly the seven canonical section headers in order [Voice, Values, Defaults, Things I keep getting wrong, Aspirations, Boundaries, Changelog], (b) total length <= 200 lines, (c) Aspirations section has at least one non-empty bullet, (d) no duplicate bullets within any section. Exit 0 on pass, 1 on any rule violation.
`claude-self lint --quiet` suppresses all stdout/stderr output and preserves the same exit code as non-quiet mode.

`claude-self default-restore --yes` overwrites the live file from `$HOME/.claude/CLAUDE_SELF.default.md` (overridable via `CLAUDE_SELF_DEFAULT`), runs lint on the result, and exits 0 when the default file lints clean. Without `--yes` exits 1 without modifying the live file (interactive confirmation is out of scope for tests).
`claude-self help` (and `claude-self` with no subcommand) prints usage listing all subcommands [show, edit, lint, log, diff, default-restore, path, help] and exits 0. Unknown subcommands exit 2.

`claude-self log` and `claude-self diff [REF]` shell out to git in the dotfiles repo (path overridable via `CLAUDE_DOTFILES`) restricted to the live file's tracked path; when the dotfiles repo is missing, exit 2 with a clear diagnostic. Implementation may delegate to `git`; the test asserts the diagnostic path when no repo exists.
Lint diagnostics on failure name the specific rule(s) violated (line-count, section-mismatch, empty-aspirations, duplicate-bullets) on stderr so the user knows what to fix.

scope: A Rust CLI binary `claude-self` with subcommands: show, lint, log, diff, default-restore, path, help (edit is a thin wrapper around $EDITOR + lint, MAY-tier).
Linter that enforces the four PRD-§4.4 rules with rule-named diagnostics.
Resolution of live-file path via `CLAUDE_SELF_FILE` env var (default `$HOME/.claude/CLAUDE_SELF.md`).
Resolution of default-file path via `CLAUDE_SELF_DEFAULT` env var (default `$HOME/.claude/CLAUDE_SELF.default.md`).
Resolution of dotfiles repo via `CLAUDE_DOTFILES` env var (default `$HOME/dotfiles`).
`default-restore --yes` non-interactive flag (testable; the bash draft's interactive `yes` prompt is preserved as default behavior but `--yes` short-circuits).
non_goals: Wiring the SessionStart hook in `~/.claude/settings.json` (out-of-scope per orchestrator brief; PRD §5 calls this out separately).
Editor integration beyond shelling out to `$EDITOR` (covered as MAY).
Cross-machine sync; `git clone` is the model (PRD §6.4).
Self-modification at runtime / Claude proposing edits without user approval (PRD §6.6); the CLI does not implement a propose-and-approve flow.
Auto-generating Changelog entries; the user (or Claude with approval) writes them by hand.

five_whys_trace.q: Why do you want a Rust `claude-self` CLI when the bash draft already works?
five_whys_trace.a: The bash draft has zero tests and silently misbehaves on malformed input (e.g. empty Aspirations section detection uses fragile awk). A Rust binary with acceptance tests is the only way the lint contract becomes a contract — without that, the 200-line cap and seven-section invariant rot the first time the file grows.

five_whys_trace.q: Why does the lint contract matter more than just having any file there?
five_whys_trace.a: Because identity is performative (PRD §8). The file's value is in being *short, structured, and rereadable every session*. If lint doesn't fail loudly when the file drifts to 300 lines or sprouts a new section, the file becomes a junk drawer and stops being load-bearing.

five_whys_trace.q: Why is rereadability per-session the hinge?
five_whys_trace.a: Today the 'how I aim to work' content scatters across `feedback_*.md` recall memories, CLAUDE.md preamble, and the user's running corrections. Each session reassembles a 'Claude personality' from those pieces and the assembly is lossy. A single canonical file loaded every session is the only way to stop the lossy reassembly. The lint contract is what keeps the file canonical.

five_whys_trace.q: Why a Rust CLI instead of just a markdown file the user maintains by hand?
five_whys_trace.a: Because past-Claude already proved this with the bash draft: the moment lint isn't mechanical, drift wins. The user wants `claude-self edit` with lint-on-save and `claude-self default-restore` so resets are recoverable. The CLI is the discipline; the markdown file is the artifact.

ambiguities_resolved.question: PRD §5 says '~80 LoC of bash' — is the autobuilder mandate to translate that to Rust, or to supersede the bash drafts entirely?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: Supersede. The bash drafts at `~/dotfiles/.local/bin/claude-self` and `~/dotfiles/.claude/scripts/claude-self-start.sh` are reference, not source-of-truth. The Rust binary is the new source-of-truth; the bash drafts remain at the dotfiles path until manually retired by the user.

ambiguities_resolved.question: PRD §4.1 'Loading' via SessionStart hook — is the CLI responsible for wiring this?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: No. Orchestrator brief explicitly says 'WIRING IS OUT OF SCOPE.' The CLI exists; wiring is a separate user action.

ambiguities_resolved.question: PRD §4.2 'Editing' requires user-approval-in-the-same-turn for Claude's edits. How does a CLI test this?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: It cannot, because the approval flow is a Claude-side convention, not a CLI feature. The `edit` subcommand is a thin `$EDITOR` wrapper; user-approval gating is enforced by Claude at the conversation layer (PRD §7 Phase 2), not by `claude-self`. Out of MUST scope; MAY-tier.

ambiguities_resolved.question: PRD §4.4 says 'failure to lint blocks a save.' Does the CLI implement a hard save-block?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: Per the bash draft `cmd_edit`, lint failure offers re-edit / accept-anyway / quit. We mirror that in `edit` (MAY). For the core lint subcommand (AC3), failure exits 1 and the file is unchanged; the calling workflow decides whether to retry.

ambiguities_resolved.question: The PRD mentions 'mirror' (PRD §7 Phase 3) integration. In scope?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: No — that's a future phase and depends on a separate PRD. Out of MUST scope.

ambiguities_resolved.question: Should the CLI handle `~/.claude/` not being a git repo (PRD §4.3)?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: Yes — `log` and `diff` shell out to git in `$CLAUDE_DOTFILES` (default `$HOME/dotfiles`) since the existing convention already has the file living in the dotfiles tree. If the repo is missing, exit 2 with a diagnostic (AC7). The CLI does not auto-initialize a git repo.
